import sys
sys.path.insert\(0, '.'\)
import matplotlib
matplotlib.use\('Agg'\)
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from mpl_toolkits.mplot3d import Axes3D
from morpho.cell import Cell
from morpho.program import GrowthProgram
from morpho.state import SimulationState
from morpho.simulation import Simulation
from morpho.interpreter import interpret, describe_program
import numpy as np
print\('All imports OK'\)

# Quick sanity-check: run 10 steps
program = GrowthProgram\(
    anisotropy_vector=np.array\([0.,1.,0.]\),
    anisotropy_strength=0.12
\)
state = SimulationState\(program=program\)
state.add_cell\(Cell\(position=np.array\([0.,0.,0.]\), radius=0.4,
                    growth_rate=0.08, division_radius=0.85,
                    stiffness=1.0, adhesion=0.5\)\)
from morpho.simulation import Simulation
sim = Simulation\(state=state, dt=0.1, damping=0.75\)
sim.run\(10, verbose=False\)
print\(f'Simulation OK — {state.n_cells} cells after 10 steps'\)
")

import sys, numpy as np
sys.path.insert\(0, '.'\)
import matplotlib; matplotlib.use\('Agg'\)
from pathlib import Path
from grow_video import Snapshot, render_frame, global_axis_limits

# Fake a small snapshot and render one frame
pos = np.array\([[0.,0.,0.],[0.5,0.3,0.],[0.2,0.8,0.1]]\)
snap = Snapshot\(positions=pos, radii=np.array\([0.3,0.4,0.35]\),
                ages=np.array\([1.,1.2,0.8]\), time=1.0, n_cells=3, step=5\)
Path\('outputs/frames'\).mkdir\(parents=True, exist_ok=True\)
lo, hi = global_axis_limits\([snap]\)
render_frame\(snap, 0, 1, \(lo, hi\), Path\('outputs/frames/test_frame.png'\)\)
size = Path\('outputs/frames/test_frame.png'\).stat\(\).st_size
print\(f'Frame rendered OK — {size} bytes'\)
")

import sys, numpy as np
sys.path.insert\(0, '.'\)
import matplotlib; matplotlib.use\('Agg'\)
from pathlib import Path

# Import the whole module to verify no syntax errors
import grow_video as gv

# Verify living cmap exists
print\('_LIVING_CMAP:', gv._LIVING_CMAP.name\)
print\('_LIVING_BG:', gv._LIVING_BG\)

# Verify tree-building preset has right values
preset = gv.PRESETS['tree-building']
prog = preset['program_override']
print\(f'stiffness_mult : {prog.stiffness_multiplier}'\)
print\(f'noise_strength : {prog.noise_strength}'\)
print\(f'adhesion_mult  : {prog.adhesion_multiplier}'\)
print\(f'div_size_mult  : {prog.division_size_multiplier}'\)
print\(f'aniso_strength : {prog.anisotropy_strength}'\)
print\(f'style          : {preset[\\"style\\"]}'\)
print\(f'n_seeds        : {len\(preset[\\"seeds\\"]\)}'\)

# Render one frame with living style
from grow_video import Snapshot, render_frame, global_axis_limits
Path\('outputs/frames'\).mkdir\(parents=True, exist_ok=True\)
pos = np.array\([[0.,0.,0.],[0.3,1.2,0.],[0.1,2.5,0.2],[-0.2,0.8,0.4]]\)
snap = Snapshot\(positions=pos, radii=np.array\([0.5,0.4,0.3,0.35]\),
                ages=np.array\([3.,2.,1.,1.5]\), time=2.5, n_cells=4, step=20\)
lo, hi = global_axis_limits\([snap]\)
render_frame\(snap, 5, 20, \(lo, hi\), Path\('outputs/frames/test_living.png'\), style='living'\)
size = Path\('outputs/frames/test_living.png'\).stat\(\).st_size
print\(f'Living frame rendered OK — {size:,} bytes'\)
")

import sys
sys.path.insert\(0, '.'\)
from pathlib import Path
from grow_video import frames_to_video

frame_dir = Path\('outputs/frames'\)
out = Path\('outputs/growth.mp4'\)
print\(f'Found {len\(sorted\(frame_dir.glob\(\\"frame_*.png\\"\)\)\)} frames'\)
print\('Assembling …'\)
result = frames_to_video\(frame_dir, out, fps=20\)
if result:
    size_mb = result.stat\(\).st_size / 1_048_576
    fmt = 'GIF' if result.suffix == '.gif' else 'MP4'
    print\(f'{fmt} saved: {result}  \({size_mb:.1f} MB\)'\)
else:
    print\('Failed'\)
")

Bash(python3 -c "
import json
with open\('/tmp/tree_data/field_survey.geojson'\) as f:
    data = json.load\(f\)

from collections import defaultdict
species_stats = defaultdict\(lambda: {'count':0, 'dbh':[], 'height':[]}\)
for feat in data['features']:
    p = feat['properties']
    sp = p['species']
    species_stats[sp]['count'] += 1
    if p['dbh'] is not None:
        species_stats[sp]['dbh'].append\(p['dbh']\)
    if p['height'] is not None:
        species_stats[sp]['height'].append\(p['height']\)

for sp, st in sorted\(species_stats.items\(\), key=lambda x: -x[1]['count']\):
    dbh = st['dbh']
    ht = st['height']
    dbh_mean = sum\(dbh\)/len\(dbh\) if dbh else 0
    ht_mean = sum\(ht\)/len\(ht\) if ht else 0
    print\(f'{sp:15s}  n={st[\\"count\\"]:4d}  dbh_mean={dbh_mean:6.1f}mm  height_mean={ht_mean:5.1f}m  h/d={ht_mean/\(dbh_mean/1000\) if dbh_mean else 0:.1f}'\)
")

Bash(python3 -c "
import laspy, numpy as np
from pathlib import Path

# Analyze all 10 plots to get per-plot crown statistics
results = []
for i in range\(1, 11\):
    las = laspy.read\(f'/tmp/tree_data/als/plot_0{i:1d}.las' if i < 10 else f'/tmp/tree_data/als/plot_{i}.las'\)
    z = las.z
    cls = las.classification
    
    # Ground points \(class 2\)
    ground_z = z[cls == 2]
    veg_z = z[cls == 5]
    x_veg = las.x[cls == 5]
    y_veg = las.y[cls == 5]
    
    if len\(ground_z\) == 0 or len\(veg_z\) == 0:
        continue
    
    ground_level = np.percentile\(ground_z, 10\)  # robust ground estimate
    norm_veg = veg_z - ground_level             # normalized heights
    
    crown_max_h = np.percentile\(norm_veg, 99\)   # 99th pct height \(dominant trees\)
    crown_mean_h = np.mean\(norm_veg\)
    
    # Crown width: spread in XY
    x_range = np.percentile\(x_veg, 95\) - np.percentile\(x_veg, 5\)
    y_range = np.percentile\(y_veg, 95\) - np.percentile\(y_veg, 5\)
    crown_width = \(x_range + y_range\) / 2
    
    h_w_ratio = crown_max_h / \(crown_width / 2\) if crown_width > 0 else 0
    
    # Point density profile: fraction in upper 30% of canopy
    upper_thresh = crown_max_h * 0.7
    upper_frac = np.mean\(norm_veg > upper_thresh\)
    
    results.append\(\(i, crown_max_h, crown_mean_h, crown_width, h_w_ratio, upper_frac\)\)

print\(f'{'Plot':>4} {'MaxH\(m\)':>8} {'MeanH\(m\)':>9} {'Width\(m\)':>9} {'H/W':>6} {'UpperDens':>9}'\)
for r in results:
    print\(f'{r[0]:>4} {r[1]:>8.1f} {r[2]:>9.1f} {r[3]:>9.1f} {r[4]:>6.2f} {r[5]:>9.3f}'\)
" 2>&1)
